frameworks.name: neural gain modulation
frameworks.one_line: ascending neuromodulators alter cortical gain to drive perceptual updating

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: trained RNN performs ambiguous categorisation; gain is manipulated in silico

frameworks.name: low-dimensional brain state / energy landscape
frameworks.one_line: perceptual switches predicted by velocity peaks and flattened landscapes

frameworks.name: thalamocortical recurrent network hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: distributed recurrent circuits whose interconnections are neuromodulated

core_question: Does phasic neuromodulatory gain modulation causally mediate perceptual switches during ambiguous-figure viewing?
key_assumptions: pupil diameter indexes phasic neuromodulatory bursts
noradrenergic tone scales effective neural gain
whole-brain fMRI dynamics admit a meaningful low-dimensional readout
manipulations: ambiguous-figure viewing eliciting perceptual switches in humans
gain parameter in a trained RNN performing analogous categorisation
key_findings: perceptual reinterpretations coincide with peaks in pupil diameter
heightened RNN gain produces earlier perceptual shifts
fMRI perceptual switches co-occur with peaks in low-dimensional state velocity
fMRI perceptual switches co-occur with flattened energy-landscape dynamics
interpretations: phasic neuromodulatory gain causally mediates perceptual switches via recurrent network reconfiguration
